question: A 45-year-old woman with a history of well-differentiated thyroid cancer undergoes hemithyroidectomy. Postoperatively, her thyroglobulin level is elevated to 100 ng/mL (SI: 100 μg/L). What is the most appropriate next step?
options: <Option A> Repeat ultrasonography of the remaining thyroid lobe
<Option B> Initiate levothyroxine therapy with a goal TSH level of <0.1 mIU/L
<Option C> Perform FNA on the elevated nodule in the remaining thyroid lobe
<Option D> Start chest CT to evaluate for metastatic disease
explanation: Given that thyroglobulin levels are elevated, it is important to first determine whether this indicates recurrence or simply a rise due to normal thyroid tissue. An FNA of the remaining nodule can help differentiate between these possibilities and guide further management.
reference: MAIN CONCLUSIONS: Thyroid hormone therapy is often required postoperatively to achieve goal TSH levels, but initiating levothyroxine with a goal <0.1 mIU/L is recommended if thyroglobulin remains elevated.
title: Surveillance of the Patient Who Has Had Therapeutic Hemithyroidectomy for Thyroid Cancer
